title: hepetis puberula
intro: Hepetis puberula, also known as lemongrass, is a tropical plant native to Asia and Africa, characterized by its long, narrow leaves and citrusy scent. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of lemongrass include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and alleviating pain, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve respiratory health. Herbal preparations of lemongrass include essential oils, teas, and infusions that can be used topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
